channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US Park Service removes transgender references from Stonewall National Monument website
item.description: The change, which has sparked an outcry among the LGBTQ+ community, came after President Trump signed an executive order directing federal agencies to recognize only two genders.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, WGN 9

item.title: Black Hawk pilots may have missed air traffic directive, flown on 'bad' altitude data before crash
item.description: It is likely the Black Hawk pilots “stepped on” the communication for less than a second by pressing the microphone key to communicate back to the control tower, she said.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justice Department moves to dismiss Eric Adams case
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally asked a federal district court to drop its corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) after federal prosecutors in Manhattan resigned rather than dismiss the case.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: More adults are ordering fast-food kids’ meals
item.description: For decades, fast-food has been America's common denominator. No matter if you were rich or poor or somewhere in the middle, you could always count on a quick, cheap meal at a burger joint, fried chicken restaurant or taco shop. Today, fast-food is still – fast.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: The Pentagon moves to take back troops discharged over COVID-19 vaccine refusals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Defense Department has told the military services to reach out once again to service members who were forced out or voluntarily left the military because they refused to get the COVID vaccine and see if they want to reenlist.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Volunteers deliver gifts to widows on Valentine's Day. The project began in a florist's driveway
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Jillian Myers knows the devastation of losing a husband and feeling alone and empty, particularly on Valentine's Day.
item.dc:creator: ERIK VERDUZCO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Helicopter crew in collision with plane may not have heard key instruction from tower, NTSB says
item.description: Investigators say the crew of the Army helicopter that collided in midair with an American Airlines jet near Washington, D.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump border czar and NYC mayor interview with 'Fox & Friends' on immigration commitments
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Reestablishing an immigration office at New York's notorious Rikers Island jail is just the first step in a more complex agreement President Donald Trump's administration has reached with Democratic Mayor Eric Adams, the new U.S.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico continues to dig for border tunnels, US watches
item.description: Mexican soldiers and police have resumed their search for illegal cross border tunnels between Juarez and El Paso.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's framed mug shot hangs outside Oval Office
item.description: White House deputy chief of staff Dan Scavino posted a video Friday showing the frame up close and revealing it is the front page of the New York Post from Aug. 25, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department asks court to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: The Justice Department has asked a court to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: American beauty queen charged in ex-NFL husband's murder plot granted release from Bahamas prison: report
item.description: Georgia mom Lindsay Shiver, who is accused of plotting to hire a hitman to kill her estranged husband, ex-NFL player Robert Shiver is reportedly being released on bail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Has Valentine's Day gotten out of hand? What readers say
item.description: Americans are expected to spend nearly $26 billion on Valentine's Day this year on jewelry, flowers, candy and dining out.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: 83 percent say president is required to follow Supreme Court rulings: Survey
item.description: Just over half, 51 percent, of Americans approve of the job the Supreme Court is doing, which is the highest amount of support it’s received in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Drone damages outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant; Russia denies responsibility
item.description: A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida man accused of luring young girls to home using pet ferrets
item.description: A Clearwater, Florida, man was charged with inappropriately touching young girls after allegedly using pet ferrets to lure them to his home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fourth federal judge blocks Trump’s birthright citizenship order
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston on Thursday blocked an executive order from President Donald Trump that would end birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally, becoming the fourth judge to do so. The ruling from U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump wants states to clean up forests to stop wildfires. But his administration cut off funds
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is holding up money for wildfire mitigation projects funded through legislation championed by his Democratic predecessor, threatening efforts to prevent catastrophic blazes like the ones that recently ripped through Southern California.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, MATTHEW BROWN and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Denver Public Schools sues DHS over policy allowing ICE in schools
item.description: Denver Public Schools (DPS) sued the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) on Wednesday over its new Trump administration policy allowing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to conduct raids in schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Alaska US senators introduce bill that would again designate North America's tallest peak as Denali
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Alaska's Republican U.S. senators have introduced legislation seeking to designate North America’s tallest peak as Denali — weeks after President Donald Trump issued an executive order calling for the name to revert to Mount McKinley. U.S. Sen.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Igloo recalling 1M+ coolers after fingertip amputations
item.description: Igloo has received 12 reports of fingertip injuries, including fingertip amputations, bone fractures and slashes.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, KRON4

item.title: Humpback whale briefly swallows kayaker
item.description: A humpback whale briefly swallowed a kayaker off Chilean Patagonia before quickly releasing him unharmed. The incident, caught on camera, quickly went viral.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Humpback whale briefly swallows kayaker
item.description: A humpback whale briefly swallowed a kayaker off Chilean Patagonia before quickly releasing him unharmed. The incident, caught on camera, quickly went viral.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What's an atmospheric river? A pineapple express? AP explains the weather phenomenon
item.description: Atmospheric rivers are a plume of moisture that extend across the Pacific to near Hawaii. When they hit land they can cause heavy rains and snow.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NIH reportedly loses two top leaders in two days
item.description: Two key staff members at the National Institutes of Health (NIH) are reportedly leaving the agency amid ongoing efforts by the Trump administration and Department of Government Efficiency head Elon Musk to drastically cut down the size of the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's pick for education chief sketches a roadmap for dismantling the department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Linda McMahon on Thursday sketched out how key functions of the Education Department could be carved up to achieve President Donald Trump's goal of dismantling the agency, vowing to “reorient” the department while continuing some of its largest programs.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man whose wife was killed in a hippo attack in Africa sues the US company that booked the trip
item.description: A New Jersey man whose wife was killed in a horrific hippopotamus attack last year during a safari in Africa is suing the U.S. company that arranged the trip, alleging it failed to ensure their safety and did not adequately screen and supervise the tour guides.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oklahoma governor says he'll stop plan to collect kids' immigration status
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt said he will stop a plan pushed by the state’s top education official to collect the immigration status of children when they register for public school.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs a plan for reciprocal tariffs on US trading partners, ushering in economic uncertainty
item.description: A trade expert claims that Trump's current tariff plan “reflects a fundamental misunderstanding of how the global economy works.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, WGN 9

item.title: US aircraft carrier collides with merchant vessel in Mediterranean Sea
item.description: The collision occurred between the Nimitz-class aircraft carrier USS Harry S. Truman (CVN 75) and the merchant vessel Besiktas-M, according to U.S. Navy spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Igloo recalls over a million coolers after handle hazard causes fingertip amputation injuries
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Igloo is recalling more than 1 million of its coolers sold across the U.S., Mexico and Canada due to a handle hazard that has resulted in a handful of fingertip injuries, including some amputations. The now-recalled “Igloo 90 Qt.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Denny's to close dozens more restaurants in 2025
item.description: Denny's was already planning to close dozens of locations this year. Now it's planning to close even more.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's education secretary may be asked to dismantle the Education Department. Here's what it does
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — If Linda McMahon is confirmed as education secretary, President Donald Trump has said he wants her to “put herself out of a job.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A free speech dispute over a New England town's pastry shop painting heads to trial
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A First Amendment dispute over a bright painting that shows sunbeams shining down on a mountain range made of sprinkle-covered chocolate and strawberry doughnuts, a blueberry muffin, a cinnamon roll and other pastries is scheduled for trial Thursday.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing 1 person, authorities say
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, Fox 5
